title: Device Section
text: Device Section The device section contains the general commands needed to drive the device. Each definition can contain up to 1024 characters defining a function mapping using the following syntax: begin_device OutputSequence UnifaceMnemonic { Description } end_device Delimiters Mnemonic Explanation begin_device Mark start of general device information. end_device Mark end of general device information. Arguments OutputSequence —sequence of codes used by the device to perform the requested action. UnifaceMnemonic —Uniface mnemonic code recognized by the device translation table compiler. See Device Translation Table Mnemonics . Some mnemonics include parameters in the output sequence that can be used to determine layout and formatting information. See Parameters in the Output Sequence . Description —optional description or comments. Except for the mode_ definitions, comments are ignored by the device translation table compiler. ^^ USYSTERM (VT220) device display and printer definitions table BEGIN_DEVICE start of device section Meta_Western Display ^esc= init select keypad ^esc[1;24r close leave terminal in 'normal state' ^esc[%v;%hH cursor(1,1) cursor posit command ^esc[?3l mode_0(80,24) normal width dimension ^esc[?3h mode_1(132,24) large width dimension ^esc[2J clear_screen clear full screen ^esc[%pX erase_line erase (part) of line function ^esc[%v;%yr^esc[%v;%hH^escM scroll_down scroll lines down ^esc[%v;%yr^esc[%y;%hH^escD scroll_up scroll lines up ^esc[%v;%hH^esc[%p@ scroll_right scroll positions right ^esc[%v;%hH^esc[%pP scroll_left scroll positions left ^007 buzz ringbell command ^FF formfeed print new page ^CR return print eol ^LF linefeed print advance ^esc[0m novideo video attributes setting ^esc[0;4m underscore ^esc[0;7m inverse ^esc[0;4;7m inv_und ^esc[0;1m high ^esc[0;4;1m hig_und ^esc[0;7;1m hig_inv ^esc[0;4;7;1m hig_inv_und ^esc[0;5m blink ^esc[0;4;5m bli_und ^esc[0;7;5m bli_inv ^esc[0;4;7;5m bli_inv_und ^esc[0;1;5m bli_hig ^esc[0;4;1;5m bli_hig_und ^esc[0;7;1;5m bli_hig_inv ^esc[0;4;7;1;5m bli_hig_inv_und END_DEVICE Copy Was this topic helpful?
